title: Use secure encryption modes and padding
description: Using insecure encryption modes or padding schemes weakens cryptographic protection. Modes like ECB or weak padding schemes can leak information about encrypted data and make it vulnerable to cryptographic attacks.
recommendation: Use secure encryption modes like CBC or GCM with proper padding schemes like PKCS#1 v2.0.

noncompliant.description: Insecure encryption modes and weak ciphers compromise cryptographic security.

compliant.description: Use secure encryption modes like GCM or CBC with proper padding schemes and strong ciphers like AES.

checks.message: Insecure encryption detected: Use secure encryption modes like GCM or CBC with proper padding, avoid weak ciphers (DES, RC4), and ensure random IV/nonce generation.
